text:
Once upon a quiet seaside town, there lived a curious cat named Whiskers. Whiskers wasn’t like any other cat—he loved wandering where nobody dared. One sunny afternoon, he trotted far past his favorite rock cave toward the old oak tree.

Inside the oak tree lived an old owl named Oliver. Oliver was lonely with his endless hours of watchfulness. One by one, every evening he’d reminisce about how his greatest thrill was spotting the moonlight dancing on the waves. But winters became bitter colder, and soon there were few stars.

That night, as stars filled the velvet sky, Oliver pondered who he might tell his tale. He envisioned spreading joy in dreams instead. And so Oliver began educating a squirrel named Sammy. Sammy learned to count stars and listen for nighttime songs. Every dawn brought new discoveries with Sammy by Oliver’s side.

Whiskers stayed away, thinking Sammy’s tales sounded boring. That changed when he heard Sammy spinning tales during a storm. Lightning cracked
